title: TPMS Service Positions and Rotation Relearn
description: Ensures TPMS sensor position IDs are correctly mapped after tire rotation. Some OEMs auto-relearn positions while driving; others require stationary tool or scan-tool relearn to update the TPMS ECU's position map.
common_symptoms: wrong tire showing low pressure warning
pressure warning on wrong corner after rotation
TPMS fault after seasonal tire swap
physical_solution: After rotation: perform correct relearn for OEM (auto-drive, stationary magnet, or scan-tool). System updates position-to-sensor-ID mapping so warnings display correct corner.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — $31 relearn initiation accessible on most OEMs pre-2019 with correct routine ID. Auto-relearn OEMs (Toyota/Stellantis) need no scan tool. GM/Honda: stationary or drive relearn, ELM327 $31 trigger accessible.
preconditions: All tires mounted and inflated to correct pressure
All 4 sensors transmitting (verify before starting relearn)
Vehicle on level surface for stationary relearn

procedure_steps.description: BEFORE ROTATION: Record current sensor IDs by position (FL/FR/RL/RR) via scan tool or TPMS tool. This is the map you're updating after rotation.
Rotate tires per vehicle rotation pattern. Install at correct positions. Inflate to spec.
For scan-tool relearn path: Enter TPMS ECU extended session $10 03. Initiate relearn mode: $31 01 [TPMS_RELEARN_ROUTINE]. TPMS ECU begins listening for sensor RF signals.

STATIONARY RELEARN (GM/Honda): Use low-frequency TPMS activation tool at each tire in sequence (FL → FR → RR → RL or per OEM). Each sensor responds to LF trigger, TPMS ECU records position. Confirm each position with horn honk or DIC message.

DRIVE RELEARN (Toyota/Stellantis auto): After initiating relearn mode, drive vehicle 10+ minutes at 15+ mph. TPMS ECU correlates sensor IDs to position via wheel speed sensor data. Light extinguishes when complete.

Verify relearn complete: TPMS light extinguished (or after 10-minute drive cycle for auto-relearn). Read sensor positions via scan tool — confirm each position shows correct sensor ID. Reduce one tire by 5 psi: warning should show correct corner.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: AUTO-RELEARN: drive 10 minutes at 15+ mph after any tire rotation or sensor change. No tool required. TPMS ECU learns positions via wheel speed correlation. Lexus: same. Confirm light extinguishes within 10 minutes of driving.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: STATIONARY RELEARN: deflate each tire 3 psi (FL, FR, RR, RL sequence) to trigger each sensor. Or use TPMS reset button (some models have button in glove box or instrument cluster). HDS scan-tool relearn also available.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: DRIVE RELEARN: most Nissan models auto-relearn after driving. Some older models: stationary relearn with TPMS trigger tool in sequence (FL → FR → RR → RL). CONSULT III+ for initiation trigger on newer.
oem_notes.Ford: FORD TPMS TRAINING TOOL required on most F-150/Explorer/Edge: use Motorcraft tool at each tire sequentially. Horn chirp confirms each position. FDRS for scan-tool relearn on 2018+. 2020+ F-150: app-based TPMS reset available.
oem_notes.GM: STATIONARY RELEARN: turn ignition ON, press TPMS reset button to enter relearn mode (DIC shows message), use TPMS trigger tool at each tire (FL → FR → RR → RL). Horn chirp confirms each. GDS2 also initiates relearn mode via $31.
oem_notes.BMW: BMW RDC RESET required after any rotation. iDrive menu: Vehicle → Tire Pressure Monitor → Reset. Or via ISTA scan tool. BMW sensors include position logic — system doesn't auto-learn positions, must reset reference pressures after rotation.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: TPMS RESET via MMI menu: Favorites → TYRE PRESSURE → Store. Or ODIS/VCDS: Tyre Pressure Monitoring → Adapt → confirm current pressures as new reference. ELM327 $31 relearn accessible pre-2019.
oem_notes.Stellantis: AUTO-RELEARN: after rotation, drive 20+ minutes at 15+ mph. Takes longest of all OEMs to auto-learn. wiTECH for accelerated relearn on 2018+. jeep/Dodge/Ram: horn chirp relearn available on most models via ignition sequence.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: AUTO or SERVICE MODE: most models auto-relearn within 10 minutes of driving. Service mode relearn: turn ignition ON, press TPMS button (if equipped) 3x rapidly, then drive. GDS2 for $31 initiation on 2019+. ELM327 accessible pre-2019.
